title: Automatically rename board and copy link to original board when using Create Board from Template automation

posts.author: YaelC (Yael  Costi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, I have a specific use case I was not able to resolve. Here’s what I’m trying to do:

I have a board of Project Requests. Each item on this board is a project request. Once the project is approved (status change), a few things happen:

  1. an item is created in an Overview Board, and the request item is connected to the overview item via connected board.
  2. a project board is created from template.

I was not able to resolve the following:

  1. change the name of the Project Board (new board) to the name of the item that was approved in the original request
  2. copy the Project Board (new board) link to the original item

What can I use to create these two requirements? It can be via plugin or marketplace app. If all else fails - is it possible through make.com automation?

Thanks in advance for any ideas

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Which product (CRM/Work Management, etc) and level (Pro/Enterprise) do you have?

posts.author: YaelC (Yael  Costi)
posts.cookedHtml: We’re on the Work Management product. we’re on Nonprofit - Pro Plan

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Got it. It will likely be available with make.com but I don’t know specifics on that.

posts.author: ngryf (Nick Gryfakis)
posts.cookedHtml:
@YaelC, have you tried using the ‘Create a board from template’ action in automations? This action allows you to customize the name of the board that is created from the template based on columns in a board.

image1201×738 41.1 KB

posts.author: YaelC (Yael  Costi)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks! This works well, missed the option of renaming the template:)
